description: Balanced Starting Positions - Download a Lichess Elite PGN dataset, extract Stockfish-evaluated balanced positions, and use them as randomized starting points in match, sprt, and benchmark-version.sh.

userStories.title: Dataset download script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script that downloads a Lichess Elite PGN dataset so that I have a local source of professional games without committing large files to the repository.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/download-positions-dataset.sh` created following the same pattern as `scripts/download-stockfish.sh`
Downloads `lichess_db_standard_rated_2016-01.pgn.zst` from `https://database.lichess.org/elite/`
Decompresses to `data/lichess-elite-2016-01.pgn` using `zstd` (or `unzstd`)
If `data/lichess-elite-2016-01.pgn` already exists, prints 'already exists — skipping' and exits 0
`data/` is added to `.gitignore` if not already present
Script is executable (chmod +x)
`bash -n scripts/download-positions-dataset.sh` passes (syntax check)

userStories.title: StockfishProcess analysis helper in chesslib
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable struct that spawns Stockfish and queries it for centipawn evaluations so that the extract-positions subcommand can analyze FEN positions without reimplementing UCI analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: `chesslib::analysis` module exists and is declared in `chesslib/src/lib.rs`
`StockfishProcess` struct in `chesslib::analysis` owns a `Child` process with piped stdin/stdout
`StockfishProcess::new(path: &Path) -> Result<StockfishProcess, AnalysisError>` spawns Stockfish and performs UCI handshake (`uci` → wait for `uciok`, then `isready` → wait for `readyok`)

`fn analyze_fen(sf: &mut StockfishProcess, fen: &str, depth: u8) -> Result<Option<i32>, AnalysisError>` sends `position fen <fen>\ngo depth <depth>\n`, reads lines until `bestmove`, returns the centipawn score from the last `info score cp <X>` line (white-positive)
Returns `Ok(None)` when only `info score mate <X>` lines appear (no cp score)
Returns `Err(AnalysisError)` if Stockfish process dies or any line read exceeds 5 seconds
`AnalysisError` implements `std::error::Error` and is convertible to `anyhow::Error` via `?`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: `chess-runner extract-positions` subcommand
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a subcommand that reads a PGN file, evaluates each position with Stockfish, and writes balanced FEN strings to a cache file so other commands can use them as starting positions.
acceptanceCriteria: `extract-positions` is a first-class clap subcommand in `chess-runner`

Accepts: `--pgn <path>` (required), `--stockfish <path>` (required), `--output <path>` (required), `--threshold <cp>` (default 50), `--min-ply <n>` (default 20), `--max-ply <n>` (default 80), `--depth <n>` (default 12), `--max-positions <n>` (default 50000)
Streams the PGN file game-by-game using `chesslib::pgn::parse_nth` in a loop (incrementing game index) until `PgnError` signals out-of-bounds
For each game, replays moves via `chesslib::pgn::replay` and visits every position between `--min-ply` and `--max-ply`
Evaluates each candidate FEN with `chesslib::analysis::analyze_fen`; keeps position only if `|score_cp| <= threshold`
Positions with a mate score (`Ok(None)`) are skipped
Deduplicates FENs using an in-memory `HashSet<String>`; duplicate entries are silently dropped
Writes one FEN per line to `--output`; stops after `--max-positions` unique entries
Prints progress to stderr every 100 games: `Games: N | Evaluated: M | Kept: K`
Exits non-zero with descriptive message on Stockfish communication failure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add `--positions-file`, `--num-positions`, `--seed` to `chess-runner match`
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to pass a FEN positions file to `chess-runner match` so that each game starts from a different balanced position drawn at random.
acceptanceCriteria: `match` subcommand accepts three new optional flags: `--positions-file <path>`, `--num-positions <n>` (default 10), `--seed <n>` (u64, default random from system entropy)
Add `rand` crate as a dependency to `chess-runner`; use `rand::rngs::SmallRng` seeded with `SmallRng::seed_from_u64(seed)`

When `--positions-file` is provided: loads all FENs from file, shuffles via seeded RNG, picks first `--num-positions` FENs (or all if file has fewer), plays `--games` games per FEN; total games = `num-positions * --games`
When `--positions-file` is absent: behavior unchanged (starts from initial position, `--games` total)
Seed is printed to stderr before any games start: `Seed: <n>`
CSV output gains a `start_fen` column; value is `startpos` when no positions file is used
Exits non-zero with descriptive error if `--positions-file` is provided but file does not exist or is empty
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add `--positions-file`, `--num-positions`, `--seed` to `chess-runner sprt`
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the same balanced-positions flags on `chess-runner sprt` so that SPRT tests exercise mid-game positions rather than always starting from the opening.
acceptanceCriteria: `sprt` subcommand accepts `--positions-file <path>`, `--num-positions <n>` (default 10), `--seed <n>` with the same semantics as `match`
Positions are cycled round-robin across SPRT games until the LLR stopping condition is met
Seed is printed to stderr before any games start: `Seed: <n>`
When `--positions-file` is absent: behavior unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update `benchmark-version.sh` to run both standard and balanced-position matches
userStories.description: As a developer, I want benchmark-version.sh to always run standard matches (from the initial position) AND additional balanced-positions matches when the cache file is present, so results cover both opening play and mid-game quality.
acceptanceCriteria: `benchmark-version.sh` accepts new optional flags: `--positions-file <path>` (defaults to `data/balanced-positions.fen` if that file exists, otherwise disabled), `--num-positions <n>` (default 20), `--seed <n>` (default 42)
The existing `run_match` calls for all four Stockfish levels (1500, 2000, 2500, max) are always executed unchanged

When a positions file is resolved, an additional balanced match runs for each level using `--positions-file`, `--num-positions`, and `--seed`; `tag_last_match_row` is called with label `<level>-balanced` (e.g. `1500-balanced`)
When `--prev-engine` is provided and a positions file is resolved, a second SPRT pass runs with `--positions-file`, `--num-positions`, and `--seed`, appending to `sprt_results.csv`
When no positions file is available, script runs exactly as before
`--help` output documents the three new flags and mentions running `scripts/download-positions-dataset.sh` + `chess-runner extract-positions` first
`bash -n scripts/benchmark-version.sh` passes (syntax check)
